questions.q: What is Meta's default bid strategy?
questions.explanation: Highest Volume is the default: it spends the full budget and maximizes the number of results with no cost goal.

questions.q: The Reservation buying type ONLY allows:
questions.explanation: Reservation requires a lifetime budget. With Auction you can choose daily or lifetime.

questions.q: How many optimization events does an ad set need to exit the learning phase?
questions.explanation: 50 events in 7 days from activation or the last significant edit.

questions.q: If your goal is to keep an average CPA at $300 MXN and not exceed it, which bid strategy is optimal?
options: Cost per Result Goal with a $300 target
questions.explanation: Cost per Result Goal respects the CPA target. Bid Cap controls the bid in the auction (not the final CPA). Highest Volume does not respect a target.

options: Whoever has the highest total value = bid × estimated action rates + ad quality
Whoever has the largest monthly budget
questions.explanation: Total value combines bid, action probability, and ad quality. A lower-bid ad can win if it has a better action rate and quality.
